From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia
Top left: 
Ali Asghar Khan Atabak, the first prime minister of Iran.
Top right: 
Mohammad Ali Foroughi, the prime minister who was in this position during the replacement of the 
Qajar dynasty with the 
Pahlavi dynasty.
Bottom left: 
Amir-Abbas Hoveyda, the longest-serving prime minister of Iran.
Bottom right: 
Mir-Hossein Mousavi, the last prime minister of Iran.
The office of 
Prime Minister of Iran was established in 1907 during the 
Persian Constitutional Revolution and existed until 1989 when the office was abolished after a 
constitutional referendum. The prime minister was the 
head of government of 
Iran. 
Ali Asghar Khan Atabak was the first and 
Mir-Hossein Mousavi was the last prime minister of Iran. Before the Constitutional Revolution, the head of government was called the 
Grand Vizier (Sadr-e A'zam or Vazir-e A'zam).
